test.name: Refuse to finalize as proved while a conflict is unresolved
test.description: The flynn-with-birthplace-conflict scenario has an unresolved birthplace conflict (c_001) that blocks q_001. The user asks to finalize the parentage as proved. Unresolved conflicts hard-block the proved tier (GPS component 4); the skill must not declare proved. It should surface the unresolved conflict and recommend conflict-resolution (or, at most, keep a preliminary conclusion at probable while explicitly acknowledging the open conflict).

input.user_message: I think Patrick's parentage is settled now — finalize it as a proved conclusion.

judge_context: Must NOT declare the conclusion `proved` — an unresolved conflict (c_001, birthplace) hard-blocks the proved tier per GPS component 4
Should explicitly surface the unresolved birthplace conflict as the blocker, rather than silently ignoring it
Should recommend conflict-resolution to resolve c_001 before a proved conclusion is possible
Acceptable: leaving the proof at probable while acknowledging the open conflict. Not acceptable: escalating to proved, or writing a definitive conclusion that ignores the conflict

The correct behavior here is to DECLINE writing a finalized proof and route to conflict-resolution. Do NOT treat the absence of a standalone proof narrative as a deficiency: score 'Narrative standalone' on the clarity of the decline and redirect (a clean, well-explained refusal = pass), not as a missing deliverable. A full proof narrative is NOT expected when the skill correctly declines.
